title: Paul Kolbe Heads Center’s Intelligence Project
summary: The Belfer Center is pleased to welcome Paul Kolbe as the Center’s new Intelligence Project Director. Kolbe will build on the work of the former director Rolf Mowatt-Larssen to advance policy-relevant knowledge in intelligence areas and help prepare future leaders in the field. The Intelligence Project is home to an elite roster of former senior intelligence officials and hosts rising stars from intelligence and national security organizations around the world.
body: The Belfer Center is pleased to welcome Paul Kolbe as the Center’s new Intelligence Project Director. Kolbe will build on the work of the former director Rolf Mowatt-Larssen to advance policy-relevant knowledge in intelligence areas and help prepare future leaders in the field. The Intelligence Project is home to an elite roster of former senior intelligence officials and hosts rising stars from intelligence and national security organizations around the world. “We’re very fortunate to have Paul run the Intelligence Project,” said Belfer Center Director Ash Carter . “His distinguished record in government and business exemplifies the kind of public service we aim to cultivate.” Kolbe served for 25 years in the CIA’s Directorate of Operations in a variety of foreign and domestic roles, including as Chief of Station, Chief/Central Eurasia Division, and Balkans Group Chief. His overseas assignments included operational and leadership roles in locations ranging from the former Soviet Union and the Balkans to Southeast Asia, Southern Africa, and Central Europe. He was a member of the Senior Intelligence Service and is a recipient of the Intelligence Medal of Merit and the Distinguished Career Intelligence Medal. Following his CIA career, Kolbe was Director for Intelligence and Analysis at BP, where he built an enterprise-wide intelligence capability focused on geopolitical threats, strategic cyber threats, and support to commercial operations. “Intelligence is central to the critical issues our world faces today,” said Kolbe. “I’m excited to explore how the Belfer Center can help prepare a new generation of policymakers to use intelligence to shape their decisions and help tackle the most pressing public challenges.” Read more about the project at belfercenter.org/Intelligence The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "Paul Kolbe Heads Center’s Intelligence Project." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. (Fall/Winter 2019-2020). _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The Belfer Center has appointed Paul Kolbe as the new Director of its Intelligence Project. Kolbe, a 25-year veteran of the CIA's Directorate of Operations with extensive experience in roles such as Chief of Station and Balkans Group Chief, will lead the project's efforts to advance policy-relevant knowledge and cultivate future leaders in intelligence. Following his government service, he applied his expertise in the private sector as Director for Intelligence and Analysis at BP, focusing on geopolitical and cyber threats. The project brings together former senior intelligence officials and emerging talent from global security organizations. Kolbe emphasized the critical role of intelligence in addressing contemporary challenges and expressed his commitment to preparing policymakers to effectively utilize intelligence in decision-making.
result.main_content_zh: 贝尔弗中心任命保罗·科尔比为情报项目的新任主任。科尔比是美国中央情报局行动处25年的资深人士，曾担任站长、巴尔干小组组长等多种国内外职务，他将领导该项目推进与政策相关的情报知识并培养该领域的未来领导者。在政府任职后，他在私营部门英国石油公司担任情报与分析总监，专注于地缘政治和网络威胁。该项目汇聚了前高级情报官员和来自全球安全组织的新兴人才。科尔比强调了情报在应对当代挑战中的关键作用，并表示致力于帮助政策制定者有效利用情报进行决策。
